rule.description: Watchguard Firewall ruleset
IPv4 source route attack was detected.
IPv4 SYN flood attack was detected.
IPv4 ICMP flood attack was detected.
IPv4 IKE flood attack was detected
IPv4 IPSEC flood attack was detected.
IPv4 UDP flood attack was detected.
IPv4 scan attack was detected.

rule.info: Name: IPv4 DDOS attack from client;
rule.description: IPv4 DDOS attack from a client was detected.
IPv4 port scan attack was detected.
IPv4 DDOS attack against a server was detected.
IPv6 SYN flood attack was detected.
IPv6 UDP flood attack was detected.
IPv6 IPSEC flood attack was detected.
IPv6 ICMP flood attack was detected.
IPv6 IKE flood attack was detected.
Traffic was detected to or from a blocked site.
IP spoofing was detected from the IP address specified in the log message.
An alarm log message was sent for traffic that matched the specified policy.
Traffic was detected to or from a blocked site.

rule.info: Name: Connection table high water mark;
rule.description: The total number of current sessions reached the high water mark (80%) of the maximum connection table.
The conntrack table is full. The number of connections has reached the configured limit.
Traffic was detected on a blocked port.
IP address %s will not be added to the blocked sites list because it already exists.

rule.info: Name: Unable to read feature keys;
rule.description: Unable to read feature keys file or fail to parse feature keys file. Features that require a correct feature key will not function.

rule.info: Name: No route to HTTP redirect host;
rule.description: Route look up on HTTP redirect host for the specified policy failed, and local HTTP redirect may not work.
The appliance sent an ARP request to verify learned ARP entry for a given host.

rule.info: Name: Possible loop or ARP spoofing detected;
rule.description: The appliance received an ARP packet sent from one of its own MAC addresses. It is possibly a network or cabling loop, or another device is faking this device's MAC address.
Firewall settings for the feature specified in the message have been updated
DNS server IP address is not yet known, device will enable DNS when a DNS server IP address is detected

rule.info: Name: Address exempted from blocked sites;
rule.description: The particular IP address is an exemption and will not be added to the blocked sites list
Firewall is starting up
Firewall is shutting down
Idle timeout has occurred for the specified blocked site, and it will be removed from the blocked sites list.
Load Balance Server status update due to response or lack of response to a TCP Probe packet. The log message specifies the server IP address and port.
Update to status of Load Balance Server due to success or failure of ICMP Probe packet. The log message specifies the server IP and status.

rule.info: Name: Feature not supported by feature key;
rule.description: The device feature key does not support the specified feature.
Detected an ARP spoofing attack. The log message specifies the source IP address, MAC address, and incoming interface of the ARP packet.
The feature key for your Application Control subscription has expired.
The feature key for your Intrusion Prevention Services subscription has expired.
Details of normal traffic either allowed or denied by the firewall policy specified in the log message.
Application Control identified traffic for an application.
IPS detected traffic that matches an IPS signature.
Record for a terminated connection
The host is blocked temporarily.
Not able to get response from specified dynamic DNS server.
The dynamic DNS server returned an invalid response code. The log message specifies that code.

rule.info: Name: The time for next update;
rule.description: The log message specifies the next update time for dynamic DNS.
Sending dynamic DNS update request. The log message specifies the size and content of the request.

rule.info: Name: Response from Dynamic DNS server;
rule.description: Receive the specified response from the dynamic DNS server.

rule.info: Name: Dynamic DNS Domain Name Resolved;
rule.description: Dynamic DNS server domain name successfully resolved to an IP address.
Connected to the specified dynamic DNS server.
Connecting to the specified dynamic DNS server.
Activate dynamic DNS on the specified interface.

rule.info: Name: Received reply from the server;
rule.description: Received the specified reply from the dynamic DNS server.

rule.info: Name: Unable to resolve domain name;
rule.description: Could not resolve domain for dynamic DNS server.

rule.info: Name: Failed to connect to the server;
rule.description: Could not connect to the dynamic DNS server due to specified reason.

rule.info: Name: Unable to connect to server;
rule.description: Unable to connect to the specified dynamic DNS server.
Add bridge interface in bridge mode.
Update ARP rules for the specified cluster role.
Fix up multipath gateways of the specified number successfully.
Update wireless settings
Update secondary IP address settings for the specified interface.
Update route settings.
Update 1-to-1 NAT settings.
Update DNS settings.
Another instance of the PPPoE process is already active in the system.

rule.info: Name: Invalid PPPoE automatic restart settings;
rule.description: Automatic restart of PPPoE is disabled due to invalid settings.
PPPoE instance will restart automatically.
PPPoE instance will not restart automatically due to no link.
Initiate gratuitous ARP for the specified interface.
Initiate gratuitous ARP for all the interfaces.

rule.info: Name: Send interface logical link status event;
rule.description: Interface status event is sent for logical link status change.

rule.info: Name: Send interface link status event;
rule.description: Interface status event is sent for link change.

rule.info: Name: A change was made to the IP address of the external interface;
rule.description: Handle IP address for the specified external interface.

rule.info: Name: Layer 2 traffic gate is closed;
rule.description: Layer 2 traffic gate is closed due to the specified reason.
Ignore unknown address operation on the specified interface.

rule.info: Name: Layer 2 traffic gate is opened;
rule.description: Layer 2 traffic gate is opened due to the specified reason.
Traffic signal is changed to the specified status.
Starting specified wireless AP.
Stopping the specified wireless Access Point.
Started to process configuration settings.
Update global bridge mode settings.
Update global drop-in mode settings.
Clean up stale connections for the expired IP address on dynamic interface.
Capture stopped due to the specified reason.
System loaded Modem process, but another instance is already active.
Updated an IPv4 dynamic route. The log message specifies the route that is changed.
Failed to retrieve license features for dynamic routing.

rule.info: Name: Not able to get license;
rule.description: Not able to get license for dynamic routing features.
Failed to parse license features for dynamic routing.
Received cluster ready event.
Received an interface status event.
Received cluster role change event.
Received a license update event.
The RCS at the specified IP address has become unresponsive

rule.info: Name: Not able to forward request to RCS;
rule.description: Not able to forward request to RCS due to no connection.

rule.info: Name: An error was detected in the configuration. The log message specifies the line number of the error.;
rule.description: Configuration error detected in %s, line %d: '%s'
Connection to RCS closed.

rule.info: Name: Not able to connect to RCS;
rule.description: Not able to connect to RCS with the specified IP address.
The PPPoE session for the specified interface is disconnected.
Shutting down the specified interface.
Interface is activated due to link-monitor success. The log message specifies the interface.
Interface is deactivated due to link-monitor failure. The log message specifies the interface.
Specified interface does not exist, The interface status is set to disabled.
The interface link status has changed. The log message specifies the new status.
Cluster is enabled and is forming.

rule.info: Name: Cluster setting changed to disabled;
rule.description: The cluster setting was changed from enabled to disabled.
The role of this device in the active/passive (A/P) cluster changed. The log message specifies the old and new roles.
The Cluster active/active (A/A) role changed. The log message specifies the old and new roles.
IPv6 interface was deactivated. The log message specifies the interface.
Shutting down specified IPv6 interface.
Initializing specified IPv6 interface.
The Wireless Access Point has been paired with the Gateway Wireless Controller.
The Wireless Access Point has been unpaired with the Gateway Wireless Controller, and will be reset to the factory default configuration.
The Gateway Wireless Controller has detected a Rogue Access Point at the given BSSID, broadcasting the indicated SSID.
If scheduled restarts (reboot or wireless restart) are enabled, this log message will appear prior to the restart action taking place on the specified Wireless Access Point.
The Firebox or XTM device failed to authenticate for PPPoE.
PPPoE stopped unexpectedly due to an unknown error.
Replaced the assigned PPPoE IP address with the configured static IP address. The assigned IP address is retained as a secondary IP address for the interface.
The specified interface established a PPPoE session. The log message also specifies the session ID, acquired IP address, and peer IP address.
Received DHCP discover from the client, but there are no free leases available.
The DHCP server offered an IP address to the specified client device.
Received DHCP request for specified IP address from the specified client.
Initializing the specified interface.
Failed to add bridge

rule.info: Name: Failed to add interface IP address;
rule.description: Failed to add the specified IP address to the specified interface.
The configuration for the cluster management interface changed. The log message specifies changes to the interface, IP address, mask and IPv6 address.
Activating specified external interface.
Deactivating the specified external interface.
Starting wireless AP service.
Starting rogue wireless AP detection scan.

rule.info: Name: Restart detecting rogue wireless AP;
rule.description: Restart rogue wireless AP detection scan.

rule.info: Name: Stop detecting rogue wireless AP;
rule.description: Stopping rogue wireless AP detection scan.
An IPv6 interface was activated. The log message specifies the interface.

rule.info: Name: No change for PPPoE IP address during cluster failover;
rule.description: PPPoE IP address did not change during cluster failover.

rule.info: Name: DHCP IP address change during cluster failover;
rule.description: The cluster completed a failover. During the failover, the DHCP IP address changed.

rule.info: Name: PPPoE IP address change during cluster failover;
rule.description: The cluster completed a failover. During the failover, the PPPoE IP address changed.

rule.info: Name: No change for DHCP IP address during cluster failover;
rule.description: DHCP IP address did not change during cluster failover.
Specified modem is disconnected.
Modem authentication failed.

rule.info: Name: Multi-WAN Domain Name Resolution Failed;
rule.description: Specified interface failed to resolve specified domain name for ping or TCP test for failover.
Specified interface did not receive a response to Probe for failover.
Specified interface marked as Failed due to no response from ping or TCP host.

rule.info: Name: Cluster role failed over to backup;
rule.description: Cluster role failed over from master to backup

rule.info: Name: Cluster role failed over to master;
rule.description: Cluster role failed over from backup to master
No valid feature key for the specified dynamic routing protocol.
Specifies the license status for a dynamic routing protocol.
The Wireless Access Point appears to be a different model than what is configured in the Gateway Wireless Controller.
A Wireless Access Point has rebooted.

rule.info: Name: New Wireless Access Point discovered;
rule.description: A new Wireless Access Point has been discovered by the Gateway Wireless Controller.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point activation failure;
rule.description: The Gateway Wireless Controller is unable to contact the WatchGuard LiveSecurity Service to activate the service and support contract for the Wireless Access Point.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point went offline;
rule.description: A Wireless Access Point has gone offline.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point firmware version change;
rule.description: The Wireless Access Point firmware version has changed.
This log is generated whenever an unpaired Wireless Access Point is automatically deployed due to the Automatic Deployment setting being enabled.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point now online;
rule.description: A Wireless Access Point is now online.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point configuration updated;
rule.description: The Wireless Access Point has been reconfigured.

rule.info: Name: Wireless Access Point Fault Condition;
rule.description: This log is generated whenever a fault condition is detected for a Wireless Access Point.

rule.info: Name: On demand scan - stage 1 completed;
rule.description: On demand scan - stage 1 completed
Specified type of scan completed
Specified type and stage of scan started
When APT server analysis result returned and identified as certain level threat, this event log will be generated to inform that the APT notification has been sent with detailed information.

rule.info: Name: APT safe result from file submission. Details='%s';
rule.description: APT safe result from file submission. Details='Policy Name: HTTP-OUT-00 Reason: clean Message: APT safe object Task_UUID: 7a1e1500e92a410fa44d907f96b9209e MD5: d2723ba60dc88ec1ea449be9eee601cc Source IP: 10.0.1.2 Source Port: 50293 Destination IP: 100.100.100.3 Destination Port: 80 Proxy Type: HTTP Proxy Host: 100.100.100.3 Path: /test.exe'
SMTP proxy -- Failed to check the specified ruleset
FTP proxy -- Failed to check the specified ruleset
The HTTP proxy cannot complete the URL because the host or URI value is missing. The HTTP request is denied.
The requested URL matched a configured URL path in the HTTP proxy. By default, all URL paths are allowed.
The HTTP chunk size line does not terminate correctly with a carriage return and line-feed (CRLF). The invalid line is specified in the log message.
The HTTP chunk size line has an invalid hexadecimal value. The invalid line is specified in the log message.

The HTTP chunk does not close with a carriage return and line feed (CRLF) because the chunk block is missing the closing characters. This is required for each chunk when chunked transfer-encoding is in use. The log message includes the invalid chunk tail line.
One line of the HTTP footer, an additional header sent at the end of a message is larger than the configured line limit. The default line limit is 4,096 bytes.
The HTTP request method specified in the Request-Line matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matched rule name and the method.
The cookie domain header matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message includes the matched rule name and the cookie domain.
The HTTP request header is missing the host value.

The authentication scheme in the HTTP header server response matches one of the configured rules, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matched rule name and the authentication scheme.
The HTTP request method does not match a configured rule. The log message specifies the method in use.

rule.info: Name: Session timeout with server idle;
rule.description: The HTTP session has timed out because no traffic has been received from the server for the specified amount of time. (Default: 10 minutes)
The HTTP header line matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matched rule name and header line.

rule.info: Name: Session timeout with client idle;
rule.description: The HTTP session has timed out because no traffic has been received from the client for the specified amount of time. (Default: 10 minutes)
The destination host name matches a proxy exception configured in the HTTP proxy.
The HTTP proxy blocked access to the destination address because of a bad reputation score for the URL.
The HTTP proxy did not complete a Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) scan for traffic to the destination address because the URL received a good reputation score.
Application Control identified the application type from the HTTP client request or server response stream.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) detected a violation of DLP rules. The log message only includes information about the first rule matched.

rule.info: Name: Session timeout with close complete command timeout;
rule.description: The Close HTTP Session command timed out because no response to the FIN packet was received within the response time limit (3 minutes).
The first line of the client request or server response is longer than the configured maximum line length. The default maximum length is 4,096 bytes.
The request line from the client does not match the standard format of [Method][SP][Request-URI][SP][HTTP/Version]. The incorrect status-line is specified in the log message.
The status line from the server does not match the standard format of [HTTP/Version][SP][Status Code][SP][Reason]. The incorrect status-line is specified in the log message.
A single client request or server response line is longer than the configured maximum line length. The default maximum length is 4,096 bytes.
The client request or server response header block length is longer than the configured limit. If maximum total length is enabled, the default limit is 16,384 bytes.
The HTTP proxy cannot process the header line because the format is incorrect. The required format is [Name]:[Value].

The HTTP footer includes additional header information that is larger than the configured block limit size. The default total message limit, if enabled, is 16,384 bytes. The log message includes information about the invalid line.
The HTTP footer includes an additional header field with syntax that violates the header format restrictions.
The HTTP content either matches a configured Body Content Type or no Body Content Type is defined (only the default rule is in use).
The HTTP header line does not follow the correct syntax for a client request or server response header. The log message contains the header line with the syntax error.
The Transfer-Encoding in the HTTP header matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matching rule name and header value.
The HTTP header Content Type matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matching rule name and header value.
The HTTP version specified in the HTTP request line matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log specifies the matched rule name and the request line.
Relative-URI is in use and the port specified in the HTTP request host header does not match the port used for the connection.
The HTTP request is sent to a web address that matched a selected WebBlocker category. The log message specifies the action taken by the proxy, the URL, and the category matched.
WebBlocker categorization failed because the configured WebBlocker server is not available. The log message specifies the profile name and a more detailed error message.
The URI in the HTTP Request-Line is longer than the configured limit. The default limit is 2,048 bytes. The log message specifies the oversize URI.
A detailed summary of the last HTTP proxy transaction.

Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion in the client request or server response header. The log message specifies the action taken, signature ID, threat severity, signature name, signature category, destination host name, and URI path.

Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion in the client request or server response content body. The log message specifies the action taken, signature ID, threat severity, signature name, signature category, destination host name, and URI path.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) detected a virus or malware. The log message specifies the virus name, destination host name, and URI path.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) failed to scan because of an error. The log message specifies the error message, the destination host name, and URI path.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) failed to scan the traffic because of the error specified in the log message.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) cannot extract data from an object because it is encrypted.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) cannot scan the object because it is larger than the configured limit. The default value varies by device type and ranges between 1 and 5 MB.

This is the configured action (allow or strip) for the HTTP proxy Range header. The default action is strip. The HTTP proxy Range header can allow partial file transfers that impact content scans because the full content is not presented.
APT Blocker detected a threat. The log message specifies the the threat level, threat name, threat class, malicious activities, destination host name, and URI path.

rule.info: Name: File submitted to APT analysis server;
rule.description: File submitted to APT analysis server for deep threat analysis. The analysis result will be notified when the analysis result is fetched from APT analysis server.
The HTTP CONNECT tunnel request port matches a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the matched rule name and port.
The HTTP Webproxy connection was redirected to a different proxy action because of the configuration setting in explicit proxy. The log message specifies the new proxy action used.

rule.info: Name: File reported safe from APT hash check;
rule.description: APT hash check did not report a threat from the object
The SMTP connection was idle for longer than the configured idle timeout limit. The default is 10 minutes.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) detected a virus or malware in an email attachment.
This SMTP audit log specifies the bytes sent, bytes received, the sender and recipient addresses, and the sender and recipient TLS cipher.
The host name in the SMTP proxy HELO or EHLO command matched one of the Greeting Rules, or the default rule of no match.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) could not complete the scan because of the error that is specified in the log message.
The EHLO response from the SMTP server includes an ESMTP option that is disabled or unknown.
spamBlocker has classified the message as bulk SPAM. The log message specifies the proxy action, the action taken, and the sender and recipient addresses.
spamBlocker has classified the message as suspect SPAM. The log message specifies the proxy action, the action taken, and the sender and recipient addresses.
spamBlocker has classified the message as not SPAM. The log message specifies the proxy action, the action taken, and the sender and recipient addresses.
spamBlocker was unable to classify the email message because of an error. The log message specifies the sender and recipient addresses.
The sender or recipient of the email message matches a spamBlocker exception specified in the SMTP proxy.
The SMTP proxy was unable to decode the email message due to the error specified in the log message.

rule.info: Name: File submitted to APT analysis server;
rule.description: File submitted to APT analysis server for deep threat analysis. The analysis result will be notified when the analysis result is fetched from APT analysis server.

rule.info: Name: File reported safe from APT hash check;
rule.description: APT hash check did not report a threat from the object

The EHLO response from the SMTP server included an authentication type that matches a configured authentication rule. The log message specifies the proxy action, the rule name, the action taken, and the authentication type.
A MIME header matched a configured rule, or the default rule of no match.
The sender address matched a rule specified in the Mail From rules.
The recipient address matched a rule specified in the Rcpt To rules.
Some of the message content matched a content filter rule.
An email attachment matched a file name rule, or the attachment is uuencoded and the SMTP proxy allows uuencoded attachments.
The email message format matched a message format rule specified in the SMTP proxy. The log message includes the error message.
Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected a threat. The log message specifies the signature name and ID, threat severity, and signature category.

The number of email recipients specified in the email message exceeds the configured limit. The default limit is 99 for inbound messages and unlimited for outbound messages. The log message specifies the proxy action and number of recipients.
The SMTP server response exceeds the configured limit. The default limit is 10,000 KB. The log message specifies the size of the response.
The email message contains a line that exceeds the configured limit. The default is 1,000 bytes. The log message specifies the line length.
The SMTP message length exceeds the configured limit. The default limit is 10,000 kb.
The SMTP message contains a header that exceeds the configured Maximum Header Length. The default is 20,000 bytes.
The SMTP request contains a command that is not supported or is not valid for the email transaction. The log message specifies the proxy action, action taken, SMTP command, and the response code.
spamBlocker has classified the message as confirmed SPAM. The log message specifies the proxy action, the action taken, and the sender and recipient addresses.

rule.info: Name: Extra pad characters in base64 encoding;
rule.description: The SMTP proxy encountered extra pad characters when the body of the base64-encoded message was processed.

rule.info: Name: Mail from address too long;
rule.description: A sender email address exceeded the configured maximum address length. The address length is unlimited by default.
Application Control identified the application in the mail message that is specified in the log message.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) detected the rule violation that is specified in the log message.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) is unable to scan because of the error specified in the log message.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) is unable to extract data from an object because the object is encrypted.

The file requested for Data Loss Prevention (DLP) analysis is larger than the configured limit. The default value varies by platform, from one to five MB. The log specifies the DLP sensor name and error message.
APT Blocker found the threat specified in the log message in an attached file.
The user name exceeds the maximum length specified in the FTP proxy. The default is 64 characters.
This log message for the FTP request transaction includes the source and destination IP addresses for the initial connections.

Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected a threat. The action configured for an IPS Match will be applied to the traffic. The log message includes the signature ID, threat severity, signature name, and signature category.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) detected a virus or malware in the attachment. The log message specifies the detected virus name and the file name of the attachment.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) failed to scan due to the error specified in the log message.
The password specified for the user exceeds the maximum length configured in the FTP proxy. The default maximum length is 32 characters.

rule.info: Name: File or directory name too long;
rule.description: The file or directory name exceeds the maximum length configured in the FTP proxy. The default maximum length is 1,024 bytes.
The command exceeded the maximum length configured in the FTP proxy. The default maximum length is 1,030 characters.

rule.info: Name: Exceeded maximum allowed login attempts;
rule.description: The user exceeded the configured maximum number of allowed failed log in attepmts per connection. The default limit is 6.

The command matched a configured rule, or the default of no match. For the FTP-server proxy action, the default is to deny any command that does not appear on the list. For the FTP-client proxy action, there is no default restriction on commands. The log message specifies the proxy action, action taken, and the command.
The file type matched a configured download rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the proxy action, action taken, and file type.
The file type matched a configured upload rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the proxy action, action taken, and file type.
The connection exceeded the configured idle time value. The default is 180 seconds.
The FTP proxy rejected the command because of a lack of required arguments, such as a user name. The log message specifies the proxy action and command.

Application Control identified an application in the FTP client request or server response. The log message specifies the proxy action, application control action, action taken, application name and ID, application category and ID, and application behavior name and ID.

Data Loss Prevention (DLP) detected a rule violation. The log message specifies the proxy action, the DLP sensor name, DLP rule name, the authenticated user, and the file name. The log message also specifies the source and destination IP addresses and port for the control channel of the FTP session.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) failed to scan because of the error specified in the log message.
Data Loss Prevention (DLP) could not scan and analyze the attachment because it is encrypted. The log message specifies the DLP sensor name, error message, the authenticated user, and the file name.

Data Loss Prevention (DLP) could not analyze the attachment because the file was larger than the configured limit. The limit varies by platform, from one to five MB. The log message specifies the DLP sensor name and error message.
APT Blocker identified a threat. The log message specifies the threat level, threat name, threat class, malicious activities, and file name where the threat was located.

rule.info: Name: File submitted to APT analysis server;
rule.description: File submitted to APT analysis server for deep threat analysis. A separate log message will appear when the result is retrieved from the APT analysis server.

rule.info: Name: File reported safe from APT hash check;
rule.description: APT hash check did not report a threat from the object
FTP proxy -- User attempted FTP bounce
The traffic was blocked because the message included an invalid number of questions.
The DNS response was blocked because the response size was less than the minimum value of 17 bytes.
The DNS response was blocked because the response ID did not match the current or previous request ID.
The DNS query name matched a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the rule matched, action taken, and query name.
The DNS request audit log specifies the query type and name.
The DNS query was blocked because the DNS query name exceeded the allowed buffer size, which varies from 0 kilobytes to 64 kilobytes.
The DNS query was blocked because the domain name was compressed.
The DNS request was blocked because the proxy failed to parse the domain name.
The DNS query was not Internet CLASS. The log message specifies the action taken and the CLASS.
The OpCode matched a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the action taken, the rule, and the OpCode.
The query type matched a configured rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the action taken, the rule matched, and the query type.
The DNS query was blocked because the query size was less than the minimum valid size of 17 bytes.
The DNS query was blocked because the query size exceeds the maximum allowed size of 271 bytes.
The DNS connection was idle longer than the configured timeout value in the DNS policy.
Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion threat. The log message specifies the signature ID, threat severity, signature name, and signature category.

Application Control identified the application type from the DNS client query and server response. The log message specifies the application name and ID, the application category name and ID, and the behavior name and ID.
The connection was idle longer than the configured timeout value. The default value is 180 seconds.
This log message specifies the IP addresses for the completed H323 call.
The media codec is denied because it matched a configured Denied Codec. The log message specifies the codec.
The header address is allowed or denied because it matches an Access Control rule configured in the H323 policy. The log message specifies the address.
Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion threat. The log message specifies the signature ID, threat severity, signature name, signature category, destination host name, and URI path.

Application Control detected an application type from the transaction. The log message specifies the action taken, the application name and ID, application category name and ID, and the application behavior name and ID.
HTTPS transaction log includes server name, certificate details and action taken.
WebBlocker identified the category for a web request. The log message specifies the category and host name.
WebBlocker failed because a WebBlocker Server was not available.
This rule log includes the matched rule name or default rule of no match and the patterns its been matched against.
The HTTPS proxy detected an invalid SSL version.
The HTTPS connection was idle longer than the timeout value configured in the HTTPS policy. The default is 180 seconds.

The HTTPS traffic was directed to a different proxy action because of the Content Inspection settings in the HTTPS proxy. The log message specifies the new proxy action used for content inspection, as well as the TLS ciphers used for the server and client.
TCP-UDP transaction log for the traffic that is configured to allow or deny.

Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion threat in TCP-UDP proxy traffic. The log message specifies the action taken, signature ID, threat severity, signature name, and signature category.
The TCP-UDP proxy recognized the protocol. The log message specifies the action taken, and the rule name.

Application Control identified the application type from the TCP-UDP proxy traffic. The log message specifies the action taken, the application name and ID, the application category name and ID, and the application behavior and ID.
This audit log message specifies the bytes sent, bytes received, and user.
The CAPA response contained the unknown or blocked capability that is specified in the log message.
Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion threat. The log message specifies the action taken, the signature ID, threat severity, signature name, and signature category.
Gateway AntiVirus detected a virus or malware in the file. The log message specifies the virus name, user, and file name.
The sender for the email matched a spamBlocker exception rule. The log message specifies the sender, recipient, and subject.
The authentication type matched a rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the rule name and authentication type.
spamBlocker classified the message as not SPAM. The log message specifies the sender and recipients.
spamBlocker was unable to classify the message because of the error specified in the log message.
The POP3 proxy encountered extra pad characters in the body of a base64-encoded message.
Application Control identified the application from the email message. The log specifies the application name and ID, application category and ID, and the application behavior name and ID.
APT Blocker found the threat specified in the log message in an attached file.
The client sent an authentication command when it was not allowed.
A POP3 header matched a configured Header rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the rule and header.
A MIME-type matched a configured content type rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the rule, MIME-type, and user name.
The attachment matches a configured file name rule, or the default rule of no match. The log message specifies the rule, file name, and user name.
The connection was idle for longer than the configured timeout limit. The default limit is 1 minute.
Gateway AntiVirus (GAV) failed to scan because of the error specified in the log message.
A line exceeds the configured limit. The default is 1,000 bytes. The log message specifies the line length.
The message is not in an allowed format. The log message specifies the error and the user.
The proxy was unable to decode and encode the message because of the error specified in the log message.
spamBlocker classified the message as confirmed SPAM. The log message specifies the sender and recipients.
spamBlocker classified the message as bulk SPAM. The log message specifies the sender and recipients.
spamBlocker classified the message as suspect SPAM. The log message specifies the sender and recipients.

rule.info: Name: File submitted to APT analysis server;
rule.description: File submitted to APT analysis server for deep threat analysis. The analysis result will be notified when the analysis result is fetched from APT analysis server.

rule.info: Name: File reported safe from APT hash check;
rule.description: APT hash check did not report a threat from the object
The connection was idle for longer than the configured timeout value. The default value is 180 seconds.
The log message specifies the source and destination of the allowed call.
The codec is allowed or denied based on the setting for Denied Codecs in the SIP policy.
The header address is allowed or denied based on the Access Control settings. The log message specifies the action taken, header and message ID.
Intrusion Prevention Service (IPS) detected an intrusion threat. The log message specifies the signature ID, threat severity, signature name, signature category, destination host name and URI path.

Application Control identified an application from the transaction. The log message specifies the action taken, the application name and ID, application category name and ID, and the application behavior name and ID.
Device auto restore from a specific image in a USB drive disc or normal restore from a normal image failed

rule.info: Name: Creating USB auto restore image failed;
rule.description: Creation of USB auto restore image failed due to %s
System backup %s%s failed due to %s.

rule.info: Name: USB auto restore failed reason;
rule.description: USB auto restore failed due to %s
USB drive format operation was %s

rule.info: Name: Reset to the default configuration failed;
rule.description: The default configuration settings were not restored after a system reset.
Exported system diagnostic file to %s successfully

rule.info: Name: Generate system diagnostic file failed;
rule.description: Generate system diagnostic file to %s failed

rule.info: Name: Periodic support snapshot is enabled;
rule.description: System periodic support snapshot is enabled
The device successfully connected to the WatchGuard Log Server designated as the primary server.
The device successfully connected to the WatchGuard Log Server designated as the backup server.
A user successfully logged in. The log message specifies the user type, user name, and IP address.
A user log in attempt failed. The log message specifies the user type, user name, IP address, and the failure reason, if available.
A user successfully logged out. The log message specifies the user type, user name, and IP address.
A custom certificate failed to generate. The default self-signed certificate will be used.
The device configuration has been changed.

rule.info: Name: Administrative accounts reset to default;
rule.description: The administrative accounts were returned to the default settings. This could be because the system is in safe mode, or because of a corrupted administrative account file.
An administrator added a feature key. The log message specifies the feature key ID.
An administrator has removed a feature key. The log message specifies the feature key ID.
'%s' feature expired. Contact WatchGuard to renew your subscription.
A feature will soon expire. The log message specifies the feature and the number of days until it expires.

rule.info: Name: Default device settings in use for safe mode;
rule.description: The device configuration was reset to the default settings because the device is in safe mode.
Authentication failed. The log message specifies the reason.
Authentication failed because both the primary and secondary authentication servers are unavailable.
Authentication failed because the specified RADIUS method is not supported.
Authentication failed because the maximum number of groups has been reached.
Device was restored from a saved backup image. The backup image was either auto restored from a USB drive or restored from another location.
System upgrade failed: %s
Upload of logo succeeded
The external authentication server is not available.
The user successfully authenticated. The log message specifies whether this is an administrative user, a firewall user, or another type of user.
User authentication failed. The log message specifies the reason.

rule.info: Name: Device configuration update with audit trail;
rule.description: The updated configuration file was successfully sent to the device from the specified Management Server. The log message indicates if the feature key was updated. The log message might also specify the revision ID and includes comments about the update.
The device retrieved an updated configuration file from the specified Management Server. The log message also indicates if device retrieved a feature key.
The IPsec certificate was successfully imported from the specified Management Server.

rule.info: Name: Management Server CA certificate import;
rule.description: The Management Server CA certificate was successfully imported from the specified Management Server.
CA certificate updated failed.
The CA certificate updated successfully to the specified new version.
The RapidDeploy package from the LiveSecurity service was successfully applied to the device.
The RapidDeploy package was not applied to the device. The log message specifies the reason.

rule.info: Name: New RSS feed update failed;
rule.description: New RSS feed from the LiveSecurity Service failed to update.
The feature key for the device was successfully downloaded from the LiveSecurity Service.

rule.info: Name: New RSS feed update succeeded;
rule.description: New RSS feed from the LiveSecurity Service was updated.

rule.info: Name: Wireless country specification update succeeded;
rule.description: The wireless country specification was successfully updated from the LiveSecurity service.
The feature key could not be downloaded from the LiveSecurity Service. The log message specifies the reason.

rule.info: Name: Wireless country specification update failed;
rule.description: The wireless country specification could not be downloaded from the LiveSecurity service. The log message specifies the failure reason and the number of retries.

rule.info: Name: RapidDeploy configuration from USB succeeded;
rule.description: The RapidDeploy configuration was successfully applied from a USB drive.

rule.info: Name: RapidDeploy configuration from USB failed;
rule.description: The RapidDeploy configuration was not successfully applied from a USB drive. The log message specifies the reason.
Valid 'LIVESECURITY' feature not found
System boot up at %s
'LIVESECURITY' feature expired (%s) prior to package release date (%s)
Shutdown requested by system
System is rebooting
System time changed from %s to %s
System is automatically rebooting at %d:%d
System upgrade to %s successful, %s
USB auto restore started
'LIVESECURITY' feature will expire on %s

rule.info: Name: Configuration reset failed during a downgrade;
rule.description: During a system downgrade, the configuration reset failed
Device auto restore from a specific image in USB drive or normal restore from a normal image
System backup succeeded

rule.info: Name: USB auto restore image created;
rule.description: USB auto restore image successfully created
The system time was changed by the NTP process.
Member Virtual Router changed state to master due to notification gap from current master
Virtual Router state changed to master
Initialization of Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) failed.
Virtual Router Redundancy Protocol (VRRP) is now enabled for this Active/Passive Cluster.
VRRP started in master state.
Virtual Router returned to initial state.
Virtual Router becomes backup due to a pause event.
Virtual Router becomes master due to a resume event.
Virtual Router state changed from master to backup
The cluster communication channel between the specified members changed state.
The specified Cluster interface is down.
Cluster member received an updated configuration from the master. The log message specifies the member serial number and configuration version number.

A DHCP server has attempted to assign an IP address to cluster member on the Cluster Interface. This log message recommends the admin isolate the Cluster interface network from the DHCP server, and specifies the interface number and IP address the cluster attempted to assign to the member.
Cluster interface link status changed to up.
Cluster interface link status changed to down.
The cluster master's attempt to synchronize time to a cluster member failed
The Virtual Router representing the cluster is now enabled
The Virtual Router representing the cluster is now disabled
Cluster was enabled on the specified member.
Cluster disabled on the cluster member while it was the cluster master.
The non-master member of the Cluster will be reset to factory-default settings because it was removed from the cluster.
Failed to reset to factory-default settings.
Full state synchronization from the master to the specified member failed. Member state will not change to Backup Master.
Full state synchronization from the master to the specified member timed out. Member state will not change to Backup Master.
Full state synchronization to the specified member was successful. Member status changed to backup master.
Cluster failover due to a link failure on the current master, which now has a health index lower than the backup master. The log message specifies which interface has the link down.
The non-master member of the cluster will be reset to factory default-settings because FireCluster is disabled.

The non-master member of the cluster will be reset to factory-default settings due to a critical configuration change. A configuration change is critical if it would cause the master and backup master to lose the TCP connection on the cluster interface.
Local member has FireCluster enabled and is ready to join.
The cluster master was unable to issue a device discovery message.
The specified member has become master.
The specified Cluster failed to receive a heartbeat message.
The master failed over to the specified member because that member has a higher health score than the master.
The cluster member changed to the specified role.
The specified member has taken over as master..
Specified monitored interface link status changed, which will change the health index for the member.
The administrator has initiated a failover.
The failover requested by administrator cannot proceed because the master has a higher health index, or the backup master is unreachable.

rule.info: Name: Failover due to interface state change;
rule.description: A cluster failover event occurred due to a change of interface state.
The role of the specified Cluster member changed.
ScanD -- Process failed to start
ScanD -- Ready for service
SIGD -- Process exiting
SIGD -- Process crashed

rule.info: Name: Signature update process failed to start;
rule.description: SIGD -- Signature update process failed to start
SIGD -- Signature update process crashed
SIGD -- License failed to load

rule.info: Name: Failed to start the signature update for the specified services;
rule.description: SIGD -- Failed to the start signature update for the specified services

rule.info: Name: Failed to check the available signature version on the server;
rule.description: SIGD -- Failed to check the available signature version on the server
SIGD -- Signature update process started
SIGD -- Signature update process completed

rule.info: Name: Signature update process for the specified version failed;
rule.description: SIGD -- Signature update process for the specified version failed

rule.info: Name: Device has the latest signature version for the specified service;
rule.description: SIGD -- Device has the latest signature version for specified service
spamD -- Failed to start
spamD -- Ready for service
The status of the IPSec tunnel changed to up or down.
Mobile VPN with L2TP updated the session for the specified user. The log message specifies the assigned virtual IP address.
Deleted a Mobile VPN with L2TP session with the specified virtual IP address.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 IKE_SA_INIT message received on wrong interface;
rule.description: IKEv2 IKE_SA_INIT negotiation failed because IKE message from peer was received on the wrong interface.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 remote gateway endpoint ID mismatch;
rule.description: IKEv2 IKE_AUTH negotiation failed because the remote ID in the gateway endpoint configuration did not match the proposed ID received from the remote gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 local gateway endpoint ID mismatch;
rule.description: IKEv2 IKE_AUTH negotiation failed because the local ID in the gateway endpoint configuration did not match the proposed ID received from the remote gateway.

rule.info: Name: Received IKEv2 message does not have expected payloads;
rule.description: IKEv2 message exchange failed because the received message from the peer does not have the expected payloads
The IKEv2 message exchange failed because the IKE proposal in the received message did not match the expected proposal.
IKEv2 message exchange failed because the DH group in the received Key Exchange (KE) payload does not match the DH-Group in the selected proposal.

rule.info: Name: Received invalid SPI during IKEv2 IPSec SA rekey;
rule.description: IKEv2 IPSec SA rekey failed because the peer sent an invalid SPI.
Dropped received invalid IKEv2 message.

rule.info: Name: Received invalid SPI during IKEv2 IPSec SA rekey;
rule.description: IKEv2 IPSec SA rekey failed because the peer sent an invalid SPI.

rule.info: Name: No response from remote gateway;
rule.description: IKEv2 connection was terminated because there was no response from the remote site.

rule.info: Name: IKE SA not found to handle IKE_SA_INIT_R message;
rule.description: IKE SA was not found to handle the received IKE_SA_INIT_R message.

rule.info: Name: Gateway endpoint not found to handle IKE_SA_INIT_R message;
rule.description: Gateway endpoint was not found to handle the received IKE_SA_INIT_R message

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 IKE SA is in deleting state;
rule.description: Received IKEv2 message was ignored because the corresponding IKE SA to handle the message was in DELETING state.

rule.info: Name: Invalid message ID in IKEv2 exchange;
rule.description: Received IKEv2 message was dropped because it has invalid message ID.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 gateway endpoint was not found to handle the received message;
rule.description: IKEv2 gateway endpoint was not found to handle the received message.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 gateway endpoint version not matched;
rule.description: IKEv2 message exchange failed because the received IKE version did not match the IKE version configured on the local gateway. Check the IKE version in the gateway endpoint configuration on both local and remote gateways.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 gateway endpoint is disabled;
rule.description: The IKEv2 gateway endpoint is disabled. It cannot be used in tunnel negotiation.
IKEv2 IKE_AUTH negotiation failed because the remote ID configured in the gateway endpoint did not match proposed ID received from the remote gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 IPSec KE DH-Group mismatch;
rule.description: IKEv2 message exchange failed because the DH group in the received Key Exchange (KE) payload does not match the DH-Group in the selected proposal.

rule.info: Name: Received unacceptable traffic selector during first CHILD SA negotiation.;
rule.description: IKEv2 first CHILD SA creation failed because the peer sent an unacceptable traffic selector.

rule.info: Name: IKEv2 peer authentication method mismatch.;
rule.description: IKEv2 tunnel negotiation failed because the incorrect authenticate method was proposed by the remote gateway.
IKEv2 tunnel negotiation failed because the local gateway could not authenticate the remote gateway.
IKEv2 tunnel negotiation failed because of possible PSK mismatch.

rule.info: Name: Received IKEv2 IKE_SA_INIT notification error message.;
rule.description: IKEv2 IKE_SA_INIT negotiation failed because the peer sent a notification error message.

rule.info: Name: Received IKEv2 CREATE_CHILD_SA/IKE_AUTH notification error message.;
rule.description: IKEv2 CREATE_CHILD_SA/IKE_AUTH negotiation failed because peer sent a notification error message.
IKEv2 IKE SA is established because IKE_AUTH negotiation is finished or IKE SA is rekeyed.
The IKEv2 message exchange failed because the IPSec proposal in the received message did not match the expected proposal.

rule.info: Name: Received invalid SPI during first CHILD SA negotiation.;
rule.description: IKEv2 first CHILD SA creation failed because the peer sent an invalid SPI.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid Main Mode second message;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of invalid second message received by local gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid Main Mode Key Exchange payload;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because local gateway received invalid Main Mode Key Exchange (KE) payload

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid main mode ID;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of invalid Main Mode ID payload received by local gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid aggressive mode hash;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because invalid aggressive mode hash payload received by specified local gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid Aggressive mode SA payload;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of invalid Aggressive mode security association (SA) payload received by specified local gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 matching aggressive mode policy not found;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation because local gateway did not find a matching aggressive mode policy.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 IKE version mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because the received IKE version did not match the IKE version configured on the local gateway. Check the IKE version in the gateway endpoint configuration on both the local and remote gateways.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 tunnel route mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed tunnel routes did not match the tunnel configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected tunnel routes.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 message retry timeout;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because an expected response was not received before the message retry timeout.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase2 message retry timeout because Phase 1 SA expired;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the Phase 1 Security Association (SA) expired.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 PFS not enabled;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) value did not match the Phase 2 configuration.
The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because an expected response was not received before the expected time.

rule.info: Name: Rejected Phase 2 negotiation due to incorrect gateway;
rule.description: Rejected Phase 2 negotiation the proposal did not use the preferred IKE gateway endpoint.
The IPSec tunnel could not be negotiated because the default IPSec certificate is not installed or is not valid.
The IPSec tunnel could not be negotiated because the IPSec certificate is not valid.

rule.info: Name: IP address not available for Mobile VPN with IPSec user;
rule.description: All virtual IP addresses allocated to this Mobile VPN with IPSec group are already assigned. New Mobile VPN with IPSec tunnels cannot be established unless existing tunnels are deleted.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 expecting main mode;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect exchange type in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the expected and received exchange type.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 expecting aggressive mode;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect exchange type in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the expected and received exchange type.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 DH group mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect Diffe-Hellman group in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the received and expected group number.
IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect hash type in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the received and expected hash type.
IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect encryption type in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the received and expected encryption type.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 authentication method mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect authentication method in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the received and expected authentication methods.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 AES key length mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of incorrect AES key length in proposal from remote gateway. The log message specifies the received and expected AES key length.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid first message;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of invalid first message received by local gateway. The log message specifies the reason.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 matching Main Mode policy not found;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation because local gateway did not find a matching Aggressive mode policy.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 remote gateway ID mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because remote ID in gateway configuration did not match proposal from remote gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 pre-shared key authentication failure;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because pre-shared key in proposal did not match gateway configuration.
IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of the reason specified in the log
Received the specified information or error message from remote gateway.
IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of no response from remote site.

rule.info: Name: Mobile user rejected - maximum user connections reached;
rule.description: Specified Mobile VPN with IPSec user connection rejected because the specified concurrent user connections limit has been reached. The log message specifies the concurrent user connections limit.
IKE phase-1 negotiation failed because no Certificate Authority (CA) certificate is available.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 peer certificate CA is not supported;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because peer certificate is not issued by a known and trusted Certificate Authority(CA).

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 received certificate with invalid CA name;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of invalid Certificate Authority (CA) name in certificate for remote gateway.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 possible shared secret mismatch;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of possible shared key mismatch.

Firebox or XTM device sent a DPD_R_U_THERE request to remote gateway, but did not receive DPD R_U_THERE_ACK response. The log message specifies the number of retries before it will delete the VPN tunnel.
The Firebox or XTM device deleted a VPN tunnel because the remote gateway did not respond to DPD R_U_THERE requests.

rule.info: Name: Did not receive KEEP_ALIVE_ACK response;
rule.description: Firebox or XTM device sent a KEEP_ALIVE request to remote gateway, but did not receive KEEP_ALIVE_ACK response. The log message specifies the number of retries before it will delete the VPN tunnel.

rule.info: Name: Deleted VPN tunnels due to keep-alive failure;
rule.description: Firebox or XTM device deleted one or more VPN tunnels because the remote gateway did not respond to keep-alive requests.

rule.info: Name: Received IKE message for unknown Phase 1 SA;
rule.description: Received IKE message for unknown P1 SA. Sending delete message to remote gateway
IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of mismatched DSS certificate ID setting.

rule.info: Name: Failed to get ID information from certificate;
rule.description: IKE phase-1 negotiation failed because failed to get ID information from certificate.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 message received on wrong interface;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because of IKE message peer was received on wrong interface.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 1 invalid aggressive mode ID;
rule.description: IKE Phase 1 negotiation failed because received ID did not match with configured ID on local gateway. Check aggressive mode ID information in gateway endpoint configuration on both local and remote gateways.
The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) value did not match the Phase 2 configuration.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase-2 proposal type mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposal did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected proposals.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 AH authentication method mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed AH authentication method did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected AH authentication method.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 ESP encryption method mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed ESP encryption method did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected ESP encryption method.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 PFS DH group mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed Perfect Forward Secrecy Diffe-Hellman (PFS DH) group number did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected PFS DH group numbers.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 ESP authentication method mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed ESP authentication method did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected ESP authentication method.

rule.info: Name: IKE Phase 2 AES key length mismatch;
rule.description: The IPSec tunnel negotiation failed because the proposed AES encryption key length did not match the Phase 2 configuration. The log message specifies the received and expected AES key length.

rule.info: Name: Received quick mode informational error message;
rule.description: Remote gateway sent an information error message in response to VPN tunnel proposal.
Firebox or XTM device dropped phase-2 negotiation because of another Phase 2 negotiation in progress.
Received notification that Extended Authentication(XAuth) failed. Aborting XAuth negotiation.

rule.info: Name: Rejected PSK authentication, Expect client XAUTH enabled.;
rule.description: Rejected proposed Phase 1 authentication method because Firebox or XTM Device expects client Extended Authentication(XAuth) enabled.

rule.info: Name: Rejected PSK authentication, Expect server XAUTH enabled.;
rule.description: Rejected proposed Phase 1 authentication method because Firebox or XTM Device expects server Extended Authentication(XAuth) enabled.

rule.info: Name: XAuth negotiation failed due to mismatched mode;
rule.description: Mobile VPN with IPSec Extended Authentication(XAuth) negotiation failed because of mismatched authentication mode.

rule.info: Name: Mobile VPN with IPSec authentication failed because of unresponsive peer;
rule.description: Mobile VPN with IPSec user authentication failed because the peer did not respond.

rule.info: Name: Mobile VPN with IPSec user connected with no group;
rule.description: Specified Mobile VPN with IPSec user successfully authenticated, but is not a member of any group.
Specified Mobile VPN with IPSec user belongs to the specified group.
IKE phase-1 negotiation was successfully completed.
The specified user logged in to Mobile VPN with PPTP. The log message specifies the user's virtual and real IP addresses.
The specified Mobile VPN with PPTP user logged off.
The Mobile VPN with SSL user with the specified virtual IP address logged out.
A user logged in to Mobile VPN with SSL. The log message specifies the user's virtual and real IP addresses.
The Mobile VPN with L2TP daemon started.
The Mobile VPN with L2TP daemon received a configuration update.
The Mobile VPN with L2TP daemon stopped.
The IPSec IKE process started.
An IPSec configuration update started.
An IPSec configuration update was successfully completed.
The device is not activated. IPSec tunnels cannot be established.
The IPSec tunnel was established or re-keyed successfully. The log message includes the security association identifiers.
The maximum allowed number of BOVPN tunnel routes have been established. No new tunnel routes can be created until active tunnel routes expire or are deleted.

rule.info: Name: IKE process -- FireCluster role changed;
rule.description: The cluster master has changed because of a FireCluster failover. The local device will not handle IKE negotiation.

rule.info: Name: Mobile security license limit reached;
rule.description: A user login from FireClient was rejected because the number of concurrently connected Mobile Security users has reached the limit supported by the Mobile Security license. The log message specifies the maximum allowed number of concurrent Mobile Security users.

rule.info: Name: Mobile security license high watermark reached;
rule.description: The number of concurrently connected Mobile Security users has reached 90 percent of the capacity supported by the Mobile Security license. The log message specifies the supported maximum number of concurrent Mobile Security users.
User has logged out of Firebox from FireClient on the device.
FireClient on the device has connected to the Firebox.

rule.info: Name: Mobile device user already login;
rule.description: User has logged in to Firebox from the device prior to the connection request.
User has logged in to Firebox through FireClient on the device.
FireClient on the device is considered disconnected due to inactivity.
FireClient on the device has disconnected.
Mobile device compliance status is Unknown. This could be because the compliance check is in progress, or because FireClient on the device is not responding.
Mobile device compliance status is Compliant, because it meets the compliance requirements.
Mobile device compliance status is Not Compliant, because it does not meet the compliance requirements.

rule.info: Name: Mobile device user session recreated;
rule.description: User session is recreated because the mobile device IP address changed.

rule.info: Name: Mobile device Authorization Agreement sign action;
rule.description: The Device Authorization Agreement is either accepted or declined by a user at the specified local time.
